docs.title: baseline
docs.text: All stats based on Level 150 unboosted creatures — no server multipliers or custom imprints. Assumes working knowledge of basic ARK mechanics.

docs.title: tier-list
docs.text: Toxic/Beta | Alpha | Electric/Volcanic/Hydro | Shadow/Fairy | Mythic/Fabled/Legendary | Angelic/Demonic | Origin | Spirit/Chaos | Nightmare

docs.title: getting-started
docs.text: Survive long enough to establish a safe location — whether it's a raft base or a small land outpost tucked away from danger.

docs.title: Early Essentials to Craft
docs.text:
Primal Forge — For smelting modded ores and materials. Crafted in inventory.

Primal Smithy — For crafting weapons, armor, and survival tools. Crafted in inventory.

WARNING: Avoid glowing-eyed creatures early on — they usually belong to higher tiers and will demolish you if provoked.

docs.title: Build Your Food & Egg Supply
docs.text:
Before taming your first modded creatures, you'll need a steady supply of vanilla crops and eggs.

Crops to Grow: Rockarrot, Savoroot, Citronal, Longrass

Egg Layers to Tame: Dodos, Dilos, other common early-game passive layers

See the FAQ section for optimizing egg production.

docs.title: toxic-alpha
docs.text:
Your first real power spike comes from Toxic-tier creatures. These tames are the gateway to stronger knockout tools and your next tier of progression.

STRUCTURE DAMAGE: N/A

docs.title: Taming Toxic Creatures
docs.text:
Weapons — Basic Tranq Arrows or a bow

Feed — Toxic Kibble after knockout

Why Tame? — Excellent early resource gathering and mobility. Toxic Blood is essential for crafting Potent Tranq Arrows.

Kill a few extra Toxic creatures to stockpile Toxic Blood for crafting.

docs.title: Crafting Alpha Kibble
docs.text:
Once you have Toxic creatures breeding, prepare for Alpha-tier taming.

Alpha Kibble Recipe — Requires unfertilized Toxic Eggs. Combine with vanilla ingredients in the Primal Smithy.

Best Early Breeders — Toxic Dodos, Toxic Dilos, Toxic Parasaurs

Keep multiple females with one male in a fenced pen for constant egg production. An Egg Collector makes this step far easier.

docs.title: Alpha Ascent
docs.text:
With Alpha Kibble in hand, upgrade your tames and set up for the Elemental tier.

How to Tame Alpha — Knock out Alpha-tier dinos using Potent Tranqs or better. Feed them Alpha Kibble.

Why Kill Some? — Defeating Alphas drops Alpha Blood — essential for crafting stronger tranqs like Alpha Arrows and Alpha Bullets.

Focus on utility creatures first — harvesters, tanks, and strong early fighters.

docs.title: elemental
docs.text:
With Elemental Kibble ready, build your elemental army and push into the first real boss fights of Primal Chaos.

STRUCTURE DAMAGE: N/A

docs.title: Elemental Matchups
docs.text:
Hydro — Strong vs Volcanic | Weak to Electric | Gains buffs near water, suffocates enemy riders and dinos in close range

Volcanic — Strong vs Electric | Weak to Hydro | Applies the Enflamed debuff, dealing burn damage over time; great for sustained DPS

Electric — Strong vs Hydro | Weak to Volcanic | Moves quickly and can stun targets; excellent for mobility and crowd control

Aim to tame at least one of each type to prepare for bosses and counter a variety of threats.

docs.title: Elemental Bosses
docs.text:
WARNING: No fliers — they will be grounded on attack.

How to Summon — Elemental Scrolls at the Primal Smithy or find them roaming wild

Boss Drops — Elemental Seeds (for rare crops), Tameable Tokens (20% drop, at least one guaranteed), Elemental Saddles

Bring multiple tames, healing potions, and be ready for sustained fights.

docs.title: Growing Elemental Crops
docs.text:
1. Plant Elemental Seeds dropped by bosses in crop plots
  2. Harvest Elemental Fruits to craft Shadow and Fairy Kibble

docs.title: shadow-fairy
docs.text:
The Shadow and Fairy tiers mark your entry into a more tactical stage of progression. Both are essential for unlocking the next three kibble tiers.

STRUCTURE DAMAGE: N/A

docs.title: Fairy Creatures
docs.text: Strong lifesteal and regeneration abilities. Excellent for sustained fights and team support.

docs.title: Shadow Creatures
docs.text:
Passive reflect ability bounces damage back at attackers. Excellent against hard-hitting enemies.

Tame both types to ensure steady egg production for advanced kibble tiers.

docs.title: Crafting Mythic, Fabled & Legendary Kibble
docs.text:
Once your Shadow and Fairy breeders are producing eggs, you can craft:

Mythic Kibble — For Mythic-tier tames

Fabled Kibble — For Fabled-tier tames

Legendary Kibble — For Legendary-tier tames

docs.title: mythic-fabled-legendary
docs.text:
You've entered the mid-game trifecta — three powerful sub-tiers that share the same level but offer different specialties.

STRUCTURE DAMAGE: N/A

docs.title: Mythic
docs.text: Excellent gatherers with 3x harvest bonus on most resources.

docs.title: Legendary
docs.text: Combat-focused with stacking kills, natural regen, bleed, and rider protection.

docs.title: Fabled
docs.text:
Utility-oriented with high base weight — ideal for hauling and crafting support.

Use these three sub-tiers to fill gaps in your lineup — gatherers, combat specialists, and heavy haulers.

docs.title: Preparing for Demonic & Angelic
docs.text:
Breed and imprint your best Mythic, Legendary, and Fabled tames for maximum stats.

Stockpile resources — Demonic and Angelic progression requires advanced crafting materials.

Farm boss tokens & rare drops — Essential for summoning higher-tier encounters later.

Upgrade saddles — Quality gear is critical for surviving Demonic/Angelic fights.

Prepare high-tier tranq ammo — These tames require significant torpor to knock out.

docs.title: Deprecated Items
docs.text: WARNING: Some items in the Chaos Smithy are deprecated. Primal Polymer and Primal Electronics no longer drop. The Demonic Rocket Launcher engram does not function.

docs.title: demonic-angelic
docs.text:
Once you've unlocked Demonic and Angelic Kibble, tame these two powerful opposites and begin the climb toward the Origin Bosses.

STRUCTURE DAMAGE: N/A

docs.title: Demonic Creatures
docs.text: High burst damage. Excellent for aggressive offense and crowd control.

docs.title: Angelic Creatures
docs.text:
Focused on healing, support, and sustain. Often boost nearby allies.

These tames complement each other in battle — one strikes hard, the other keeps your team alive.

docs.title: Berserk Mode
docs.text:
Demonic Berserk — Boosts Speed, Torpor Output, Damage, AOE Damage. Sets nearby enemies on fire.

Angelic Berserk — Boosts Speed, Torpor Output, Health Regen, Max Health. Activates Allied Damage Absorption (redirects ALL incoming damage from nearby tames to the Angelic).

Activation — PC: Press X | Console: Click Right Thumbstick

WARNING: Wild Demonics auto-activate Berserk Mode below 40% health, doubling their torpor.

docs.title: Hunt Primal Creatures
docs.text:
To advance, you'll need Primal Blood — a key ingredient for crafting Origin Tokens.

Only true Primal creatures drop Primal Blood — No drops from Tek or Resurrected variants.

WARNING: Primal creatures apply a Grounded Debuff — flying is completely disabled.

docs.title: Defeat Origin Creatures
docs.text:
Slaying Origin creatures grants Origin Tokens needed to summon Demonic and Angelic bosses.

Each Origin Kill Rewards — Origin Token, High-tier materials

WARNING: These fights are tough — bring fully imprinted, buffed Demonic or Angelic tames with quality saddles and healing potions.

docs.title: Boss: Skorvax the Demonking
docs.text:
Demonic Boss summoned using Origin Tokens.

Drops — Chance for Tameable Token, Saddle (craftable in Primal Smithy), higher quality saddles from Ascendant Lootboss

docs.title: Boss: Draconis (Origin Boss)
docs.text:
The most powerful Origin Boss, summoned with crafted scrolls at the Primal Smithy.

Phase Mechanics — Activates invulnerable shield at ~90%, ~50%, and ~10% HP. Kill summoned minions to break shields.

Tameable Draconis — Chance to drop Tameable Token. Requires Origin Artifact from Mr. Artifact the Original.

Dodoreapers take 80% reduced damage unless affected by Charged Light — bring a Glowtail or other charge pet.

docs.title: spirit-chaos
docs.text:
Spirit and Chaos creatures represent the pinnacle of standard taming progression. These powerful tames unlock your path to Origins and beyond.

STRUCTURE DAMAGE: N/A

docs.title: Tame Spirit & Chaos Creatures
docs.text:
Defeat the Spirit and Chaos bosses (summoned at the Primal Smithy) to unlock the ability to tame their wild counterparts. Each boss unlocks their respective kibble engram.

These are among the strongest creatures in the game. Build your final boss team around them.

docs.title: Spirit Creatures
docs.text: High damage output with excellent mobility. Spirit tames excel at quick strikes and repositioning.

docs.title: Chaos Creatures
docs.text:
Devastating AOE attacks and crowd control. Chaos tames dominate in prolonged fights against multiple enemies.

Use Spirit and Chaos kibble to prepare for Origin encounters.

docs.title: origins
docs.text:
Origins are elite-tier creatures that bridge the gap between standard progression and the ultimate Nightmare tier.

STRUCTURE DAMAGE: N/A

docs.title: Taming Origins
docs.text:
Tameable Origins can only be summoned using Tameable Tokens, dropped by Draconis minions (50% chance).

Taming Process:

  1. Weaken to 20% health before torpor will affect them
  2. Keep them below 20% until fully knocked out (they heal quickly)
  3. Requires an Origin Artifact to summon (dropped by Mr. Artifact the Original)

docs.title: Origin Passive Buffs
docs.text:
Self — 30% resistance and 10% healing

Allies — 10% resistance and 5% healing

Origins are powerful support tames that buff your entire army.

docs.title: nightmares
docs.text:
Nightmares represent the ultimate tier of Primal Chaos — devastating creatures that can challenge even the Deathknight.

STRUCTURE DAMAGE: N/A

docs.title: Hunt Primal Resurrected Creatures
docs.text:
Powerful untamable enemies designed to test endgame strength.

Main Drop — Primal Bones (used to craft Nightmares and Deathknights)

WARNING: Grounded Debuff — fliers disabled. Extremely high damage.

docs.title: Nightmare Bosses
docs.text:
Required for unlocking the Deathknight and obtaining Nightmare Tokens.

How to Encounter — Summon using crafted Nightmare Summons (Origin Tokens + Primal Bones) or rare wild spawns.

Drops — Nightmare Token (required for Deathknight), high-end tools, armor, and rare mats.

docs.title: Evolving Origins to Nightmares
docs.text:
Craft the Nightmare Evolution item inside the Origin's inventory.

Required Materials — Angelic Soul x5, Demonic Soul x5, Nightmare Token x1, Origin Token x1, Primal Blood x300, Primal Soul x1, Tameable Origin Summon x1, Chaos Kibble x1, Fabled Blood x500, Origin Blood x5, Origin Soul x1, Primal Bones x100, Spirit Kibble x1

WARNING: Your Origin will lose its resistance and healing passives, but gain even greater power as a Nightmare!

docs.title: Boss: Deathknight
docs.text:
The final challenge of Primal Chaos progression.

Summon — Crafted using Nightmare Tokens + rare materials

Fight Mechanics — Takes reduced damage from all sources. Nightmares are your key DPS; Angelics in Berserk Mode can soak massive hits with damage absorption.

Drops — DK Armor (unbreakable), DK Saddle, Demonic Rocket Launcher (novelty), Tameable Token (10% chance)

Enter this fight with coordination, healing, and max-tier gear — every step of your journey leads to this moment.

docs.title: loot-bosses
docs.text: Loot Bosses provide progressively better rewards as they evolve through tiers.

docs.title: Evolution Mechanic
docs.text:
Kill a Loot Boss to guarantee its evolution. The upgraded version spawns near the dead body and will be passive (unless you stand right next to it).

Persistence — Maximum of 6 Loot Bosses can exist on the map at once.

Drops — Only Loot Bosses drop saddles. Drops scale upward — each higher tier inherits the saddle drop pool of all tiers below it.

docs.title: Loot Boss Tiers
docs.text:
Ramshackle — Spawns naturally in the wild. Kill Mesopithecus and Procoptodon to encourage spawns.

Apprentice — Evolved from Ramshackle

Journeyman — Evolved from Apprentice

Mastercraft — Evolved from Journeyman

Ascendant — Drops Demonic and Angelic Saddles

Origin — Goes berserk randomly during combat. Drops quality Origin Saddles.

Nightmare — Goes berserk randomly during combat. Drops quality Nightmare Saddles.

docs.title: Mr. Artifact the Stronger
docs.text:
Basic version. Drops useful artifacts. Great early game option for gathering boss requirements.

Location — Found anywhere Megatheriums spawn. Kill megatheriums to encourage spawns.

Persistence — Max 6 Mr. Artifacts will persistently exist on the map.

docs.title: Mr. Artifact the Original
docs.text:
30% chance to spawn when you defeat a regular Mr. Artifact.

Drops — More artifacts and high chance to drop Origin Artifact blueprint.

Origin Artifact — Used to tame Origins.

WARNING: Has punishing attacks — requires careful dodging and observation.

docs.title: faq
docs.text:
How do dinos spawn? Modded creatures piggyback from vanilla dino spawns. A vanilla Argent spawning might result in a Beta Argent or Alpha Argent instead.

How do I get unfertilized eggs for kibble? Set layegginterval to 0.3 in GAME INI. Ensure dinos are mate boosted (1 male + females). Disable mating (unfertilized eggs needed). Use an OviRaptor for egg-laying boost.

Why aren't dinos spawning? Move the mod to 1st/top of mod order, then perform a dino wipe.

Can we take modded dinos into boss arenas? If their vanilla counterpart can enter, the modded version also can. Note: Due to size changes at higher tiers, some dinos become too large to enter the Tek Cave.

How do we unlock tek engrams or get element? Primal Chaos doesn't provide custom ways — use vanilla Ark mechanics.

Elemental Titans? Removed from the mod indefinitely.

Omega Dinos? Removed from the mod indefinitely.

docs.title: Tool Harvesting Multipliers
docs.text:
Mythic Tools — 3x harvesting

Elemental Tools — 2.5x harvesting

Alpha Tools — 2x harvesting

Toxic Tools — 1.5x harvesting

docs.title: Tranq Ammo Values
docs.text:
Tranq Darts (Longneck) — Potent 880-1.9k | Alpha 1.7k-3.8k | Elemental 2.6k-6.4k | Mythic 5.5k-9.2k

Tranq Bullets (Sniper) — Potent 2k | Alpha 4.5k | Elemental 8.5k | Mythic 13k | Primal 20k (boss drop only)

Compound Tranq Arrows — Potent 3k | Alpha 6k | Elemental 12k | Mythic 17k | Primal 30k (boss drop only)

docs.title: Primal Nemesis — Guide Content
docs.text: Edit this file to update the guide. Use ## for section headers, ### for subsections. Tips use > blockquote. Warnings use > ⚠️ WARNING: text.

docs.title: baseline
docs.text: All stats based on Level 150 unboosted creatures — no server multipliers or custom imprints. Assumes working knowledge of basic ARK mechanics.

docs.title: tier-list
docs.text:
Alpha | Alpha Skeletal | Apex | Apex Skeletal | Storm | Volcanic | Frost | Nature | Earth | Water | Plasma | Ghost | Plague | Bionic | Demonic | Celestial | Godly

Water Tiers (Non-Progression): Pacific — Alpha equivalent | Atlantic — Apex equivalent

docs.title: getting-started
docs.text: Build core crafting structures and prepare for early hunts.

docs.title: Core Crafting Structures
docs.text:
Primal Forge — Smelts metal and alloys. Crafts advanced ingots (Copper, Tin). Required for Zinc Ore.

Primal Cauldron — Brews custom kibbles, potions, dyes, and consumables.

Primal Bench — Primary station for modded gear, kibble, and boss item crafting.

docs.title: Loot System
docs.text: All loot rolls randomized stat bonuses — armor, weapons, tools, saddles, rings, and dino totems. Sources: Loot Bosses, Quests, Wild dino kills. Higher tiers provide more stat slots and stronger values. Quality ranges from Common through Ancient.

docs.title: alpha
docs.text:
Proximity buff increases damage and resistance for nearby dinos.

STRUCTURE DAMAGE: Wood

docs.title: Gear Up
docs.text:
Weapons — Primal Spear, Bow, Tranq Arrows

Utility — Primal Parachute, Primal Grappling Gun

Alpha Blood — Used to craft Alpha-tier tranq ammo

Alpha Hide — Used for essential items such as the Primal Bed

Alpha tames provide the Alpha Buff — doubles damage and resistance for all nearby tamed dinos. Keep one nearby during any boss fight.

docs.title: First Tames
docs.text: Once Alpha Kibble is available from egg farms, prioritize: Pteranodons for travel and scouting. Parasaurs, Trikes, or Stegos for berry farming.

docs.title: Boss: The Alpha Boss
docs.text:
Primary progression gate of the Alpha tier. Defeating it unlocks Apex Kibble and the Primal Incubator.

Preparation — Craft summon at Primal Bench. Use a secure enclosed arena. Bring saddled dinos and Alpha Health Potions.

Phase 1 — Defeat the Alpha Boss.

Phase 2 — Immediate respawn as Skeletal Alpha Boss. Both phases required for full rewards.

Rewards — Alpha Boss Skull | Unlocks Apex Kibble | Unlocks Primal Incubator

docs.title: Skeletal and Boss Variants
docs.text: Skeletal variants require Alpha/Apex Boss Kibble to tame and drop Keratin and Blood. Boss variants are stronger, drop Element, and can be bred.

docs.title: apex
docs.text:
Increased strength and durability over Alpha tier.

STRUCTURE DAMAGE: Stone

docs.title: Bloodrage Mechanic
docs.text: Apex creatures gain Bloodrage during combat — each kill increases damage output and provides health regeneration over time.

docs.title: Advancing to Apex
docs.text: Target Rexes, Spinos, and other high-DPS Apex dinos. Replace weaker Alpha tames with higher-stat Apex variants.

docs.title: Boss: The Apex Troll
docs.text:
First boss encounter in this tier. Defeating it unlocks Elemental Kibble.

⚠️ WARNING: Boss fights apply a Grounded Debuff — disables flight, abilities, and mounting. No fliers.

docs.title: Apex Feather Farm
docs.text: Establish a reliable feather source before progressing — required for mid- to late-game crafting and kibble. Sources: Argentavis, Dodo, Terror Bird, Archaeopteryx. Apex Dodos are the easiest starting option.

docs.title: elemental
docs.text:
Exploit elemental strengths and weaknesses — team composition is critical.

STRUCTURE DAMAGE: Stone

Any Elemental Blood can be used interchangeably — blood from any elemental type satisfies requirements calling for Elemental Blood.

docs.title: Elemental Kibble
docs.text: Elemental creatures drop their respective cores (e.g. Volcanic Core, Hydro Core). In the Primal Cauldron: 1 Core → 10 Essence. Elemental Essence crafts Elemental Kibble.

docs.title: Elemental Matchups
docs.text:
Storm — Strong vs Hydro | Weak to Earth | Mobile power generation, scales range with melee

Volcanic — Strong vs Frost | Weak to Hydro | Cook meat in inventory, mobile forge, temperature resist

Hydro — Strong vs Volcanic | Weak to Storm | Water enhancement, store water, reduce O2 drain nearby

Frost — Strong vs Nature | Weak to Volcanic | Food preservation like a fridge, temperature resist

Nature — Strong vs Earth | Weak to Frost | Crop growth boost, healing AoE, 150% greenhouse effect

Earth — Strong vs Storm | Weak to Nature | Crafting speed boost, rock shield for nearby allies

⚠️ WARNING: Nature dinos provide a 150% greenhouse effect that REPLACES existing greenhouse bonuses. Do not use near a full greenhouse (300%) — it will reduce efficiency.

docs.title: Base Pairing Strategy
docs.text:
Storm + Hydro — Power generation + water supply

Earth + Nature — Faster crafting + improved crop production

Frost + Volcanic — Full temperature control across all environments

docs.title: Boss: Elemental Kaiju Bosses
docs.text:
Can be crafted at the Primal Bench and challenged in any order.

Preparation — Fully saddled and healed tames. Stock Alpha and Nature Potions. Build a team of evolved Titans with high Health and Melee.

Rewards — Boss Skull | Elemental Fruit Seeds | Elemental Boss Essence

docs.title: plasma-ghost-plague
docs.text:
Only the Plague Boss is required to progress to Bionic.

STRUCTURE DAMAGE: Stone

docs.title: Type Triangle
docs.text: Plague beats Plasma | Plasma beats Ghost | Ghost beats Plague

docs.title: Plasma
docs.text:
Creatures composed of unstable plasma energy. Plasma beats Ghost. Immune to projectiles. Weak against Plague.

New Tools: Plasma Netgun (traps Ghosts) | GhostBuster 9000 (KO tool)

Boss: Plasma Boss — Beware the shield, keep your distance and dodge all attacks. Can summon Minions similar to the vanilla Overseer fight.

⚠️ WARNING: Huge health pool. Come fully stocked on potions.

docs.title: Ghost
docs.text:
Spectral aquatic creatures with powerful nighttime advantages. Immune to projectiles and normal tranqs. Can fly out of water. Extremely strong at night.

Taming — Plasma Netgun to trap | GhostBuster 9000 to KO

Boss: Ghost Boss — Flyers only. Must be fought with flying creatures. Ground tames not viable. Weak to Plasma. Ghosts strongest at night.

⚠️ WARNING: Non-flyer strategies will fail. Bring strong fliers — boss has a large health pool.

docs.title: Plague
docs.text:
Creatures influenced by virulent corruption. Plague beats Plasma. Spreads a virus on contact — short-duration damage-over-time debuff.

Boss: Plague Boss — Defeating unlocks Bionic Kibble.

docs.title: bionic
docs.text: STRUCTURE DAMAGE: Stone

docs.title: Boss Chain
docs.text:
Defeat bosses to acquire both Bionic Skulls. Then craft the Bionic God Scroll at the Primal Bench to unlock the Scion of Darkness.

Victory Conditions — High DPS for boss phase | Fast tower-breakers for shield phase | Strong mobility to dodge plasma fire

Rewards — Bionic Boss Skull | Bionic Boss Essence | Unlocks Primal Bionic Forge

docs.title: Primal Bionic Forge
docs.text:
Unlocked by defeating the Bionic God. Only station that can refine Mithril Ore → Mithril Ingots and Gold Ore → Gold Ingots.

Power Requirements — Storm Essence | Volcanic Essence | Or a nearby Storm creature

docs.title: celestial-demonic
docs.text: STRUCTURE DAMAGE: Tek (AoE attacks)

docs.title: The Hunt for Honey
docs.text:
To craft Demonic and Celestial Kibble, hunt Demonic Bees and Celestial Bees in the wild.

⚠️ WARNING: These bees hit hard — bring your best gear or prepare to respawn.

Required — Strong Bionic-tier tame | Fabricated Sniper Rifle | High-tier Bionic+ Tranqs

Two paths — Full Assault (kill directly) or Tranq and Finish. Both yield 2 Honey per bee.

docs.title: Tame Your First Bees
docs.text:
KO and tame Demonic and Celestial Bees. Convert them into a Beehive via radial menu and insert Celestial or Demonic Flowers for passive honey production.

Tame as many variants as possible — each has unique abilities, and some are game-changers.

docs.title: Boss: Demonic and Celestial Bosses
docs.text:
Prep — Bring strongest Bionic/Demonic/Celestial creatures with high-quality saddles and imprints. Stockpile Alpha Health Potions and Nature Buffs. Use Alpha Buffs mid-fight.

Rewards — Demonic / Celestial Boss Skull | Boss Essence | Celestial and Demonic Seeds (for Godly Kibble)

docs.title: godly
docs.text:
Health potions recommended due to high stats and projectile damage.

Six Godly Bosses roam the map — only one of each at a time. You will be notified when one spawns. On death each boss summons a tameable version of itself.

docs.title: Boss: Nemesis — The Godly Boss
docs.text:
Collect tokens from all six Godly Bosses to summon Nemesis — the ultimate early-game fight. Conquer it to complete the early-game arc.

Rewards — Godly Potions | Max XP Potions | Godly Boss Essence | Godly Boss Skull

docs.title: loot-bosses
docs.text:
Rare enemies that spawn naturally in the wild across all tiers. Project a red beam into the sky — when attacked, the beam disappears to signal engagement. Drops go into inventory, not on the ground.

Saddles — Crafted saddles are always Primitive. Loot bosses are the primary source of higher-quality saddles with randomized stats. Can drop finished saddles and blueprints.

Tame Helpers — Basic Tame Helper (x10) craftable in Primal Cauldron. Stronger versions are exclusive loot boss drops. Alpha and Nature potions craftable; Plasma, Celestial, and Godly potions are exclusive drops.

Quests — Common Resource Quests triggered by killing creatures. High-Level Quests dropped by Imposter Loot Bosses (Alpha, Apex, Elemental, Bionic).

Quest scrolls must be consumed from a dino's inventory. Quest UI is only visible while riding that specific dino.

docs.title: loot-system
docs.text:
All dropped loot rolls randomized stat bonuses — similar to MMO loot systems.

Stat slots by tier: Alpha 2 slots Low | Apex 3 slots Medium | Elemental+ More slots Higher values | Godly Most slots Highest values

Loot Quality: Common → Uncommon → Rare → Epic → Legendary → Divine → Ancient

Ancient only obtainable through quests. Divine is the new high-end tier above Legendary.

Unique Weapons — Zeus Spear (throws lightning bolts where it lands) | Ragnarok Spear (unleashes nukes or meteors on impact)

docs.title: Nickel Ore
docs.text:
Source: Resource Rock Golem

Hover above a Resource Rock Golem with a flying mount to bait it into spawning Minions. Defeat and harvest Minions using a Modded Dino (Apex Rex recommended). Kill and harvest the Golem — Modded Tools are bugged, use Modded Dinos.

Look for Resource Rock Golems that show "Can be tamed with Modded Ore" — tame one and use its ability to spawn your own Minions for unlimited Nickel.

Taming the Golem — Griffin hover and shoot straight down with Rocket Launcher aimed at the head. Or trap and use a Cannon for Torpor. Captain Reptile's Cannon mod can help.

docs.title: Starfire Metal / Mithril / Gold
docs.text:
Source: DemiGod StarFire Golem

When fighting a Resource Rock Golem there is a 25% chance it respawns as a DemiGod StarFire Golem. During the fight it periodically summons meteors — harvest these for Starfire Metal, Mithril Ore, and Gold Ore. Each meteor lasts 7 minutes.

The meteor ability fires every 3 minutes. Keep the DemiGod alive to farm multiple waves — the most efficient method for gathering these ores.

Refining — Mithril and Gold Ore can only be smelted in the Primal Bionic Forge. Requires Storm Essence, Volcanic Essence, or a nearby Storm creature.

docs.title: Rice / Kelp / Sushi
docs.text:
Use a Berry Gatherer (Trike, Bronto, Parasaur) to collect Rice and Seaweed seeds from Berry Bushes. Plant in greenhouse. For Prime Fish Meat target Basilosaurus, Deinosuchus, Mosasaurus, or Spine Croc with Pickaxe or Chainsaw.

⚠️ WARNING: Prime Fish Meat does NOT stack — plan inventory space accordingly.

Combine Prime Fish Meat, Rice, and Kelp in the Primal Cauldron. Cauldron holds 50 items max.

docs.title: charms
docs.text: All enhancers roll a value within a range when obtained — two items of the same type can have different strength levels.

docs.title: Player Ring System
docs.text: Rings are modular equipment with insertable charms. Ring tiers: Common → Uncommon → Rare → Epic → Legendary → Godly. Common rings start with 1 charm slot; each tier adds capacity.

docs.title: Enhancer Types
docs.text:
XP — Player and Dino — Increases experience gain

Weight — Player and Dino — Increases carrying capacity

Speed — Player and Dino — Increases movement speed

Healing — Player and Dino — Gradual health regeneration

Dodge — Player and Dino — Chance to evade incoming damage

Timestop — Player and Dino — Stops time briefly when triggered

Gravity — Player and Dino — Reduces gravity for higher jumps

Fall Damage — Player and Dino — Reduces fall damage

Crafting — Player only — Reduces crafting time and cost

Heat — Player only — Reduces heat effects

Baby — Dino only — Affects baby growth and maturation

docs.title: How to Tame a Titan
docs.text:
1. Defeat the Elemental Boss — Unlocks the Elemental Incubator Engram and drops the Boss Skull (required for Whelpling recipe)

  2. Hunt a Wild Elemental Youngling — Cannot be crafted or summoned. On kill automatically drops an Elemental Heart into your inventory.

  3. Place Incubator strategically — Keep one at base for recipes (DO NOT hatch from it) and one in an open area for the actual hatch.

  4. Craft the Whelpling — Spawns immediately as a wild creature. KO and feed matching Elemental Kibble.

⚠️ WARNING: Younglings hit HARDER than Elemental Bosses. Come fully geared with your best tames. Do NOT summon a Whelpling inside your base — it will destroy structures.

docs.title: Titan Evolution
docs.text:
Whelpling — Baby/juvenile version

Youngling — Use evolution item in inventory. Similar strength to wild variant.

Adult Titan — Gains ALL special attacks from boss variant. Ideal for Elemental boss fights.

docs.title: faq
docs.text:
How do dinos spawn? Modded creatures piggyback from vanilla dino spawns. A vanilla Argent spawning might result in an Alpha Argent or Apex Argent instead.

How do I get unfertilized eggs for kibble? Set layegginterval to 0.3 in GAME INI. Ensure dinos are mate boosted (1 male + females). Disable mating (unfertilized eggs needed). Use an OviRaptor with egg-boost buff. Stay in render range unless using a mod.

Why aren't modded dinos spawning? Move the mod to 1st/top of mod order then perform a dino wipe.

Can we take modded dinos into boss arenas? If their vanilla counterpart can enter the modded version can also. Note: Due to size increases at higher tiers some dinos become too large to enter the Tek Cave (e.g. Elemental Rexes).

What are Colossal and Pygmy traits? Both have faster movement and increased melee damage. Colossal — Higher base health and melee, suited for tanking. Pygmy — 10% chance to dodge incoming attacks, effective in long fights. Can be applied via trait items or appear randomly through breeding.

What are the new dino traits? Nuclear — Builds an internal timer when aggressive or taking damage. Detonates in a nuclear explosion at zero. When tamed triggers below 10% health. Volatile — Explodes on death, both wild and tamed. Nightmare — Doubled stats during nighttime. Nighthunter — Extremely aggressive at night with increased aggro range. Chunky — Increased harvest health, yields more materials when harvested.

How does the quality ranking system work? Common → Uncommon → Rare → Epic → Legendary → Divine → Ancient. Divine is the new high-end tier above Legendary. Ancient only obtainable through quests.

Tranq Ammo Values:

Ammo Type Alpha Apex Elemental Demonic/Celestial Tranq Darts (Longneck) 1.1k–1.9k 1.9k–5.7k 4.6k–15.4k 23k–85k Tranq Bullets (Sniper) 1.2k–4.4k 2.4k–8.2k 5k–17.9k 25k–87.5k Compound Tranq Arrows 1.5k–6.9k 3k–10.7k 5.9k–22.9k 30k–97.5k

docs.title: RokiArkNet Plugins
docs.text: Custom ARK: Survival Ascended server plugins for enhanced gameplay.

docs.title: Available Plugins
docs.text: Plugin Description License EngramControl Block engrams and unlock via boss kills Annual DinoLoot Add custom loot drops to dino corpses Annual TekUnlock Unlock Tek engrams by killing bosses Annual DinoLevelOverride Override wild dino level distributions Free RiderProtection Protect mounted players from damage Free ItemExchange Exchange items between players Free

docs.title: Installation
docs.text:
1. Download the plugin zip from the releases page
  2. Extract to ShooterGame/Binaries/Win64/ArkApi/Plugins/
  3. Restart server or use hot-reload (.ArkApi extension)

docs.title: Licensing
docs.text:
Premium plugins require an annual license key. Purchase at ko-fi.com/rokiarknet.

Free plugins have no restrictions.

docs.title: Support
docs.text:
• Discord: RokiArkNet Discord
  • Issues: GitHub Issues

docs.title: DinoLevelOverride
docs.text: Override wild dino level distributions for custom spawning.

docs.title: Features
docs.text:
• Custom Level Ranges - Set min/max levels for wild dinos
  • Per-Dino Settings - Different ranges for different species
  • Weighted Distribution - Control level distribution curves

docs.title: Commands
docs.text: Command Description DinoLevelOverride.Reload Reload configuration

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text:
{
  "Settings": {
    "Enabled": true,
    "DefaultMinLevel": 1,
    "DefaultMaxLevel": 150
  },
  "Overrides": {
    "Rex_Character_BP": {
      "MinLevel": 50,
      "MaxLevel": 300
    },
    "Alpha_": {
      "MinLevel": 150,
      "MaxLevel": 500
    }
  }
}

docs.title: License
docs.text: Free - No license required.

docs.title: DinoLoot
docs.text: Add configurable loot drops to dino corpses when killed.

docs.title: Features
docs.text:
• Custom Loot Pools - Define what items drop from each dino type
  • Quality Ranges - Set min/max quality for item drops
  • Blueprint Chance - Configure chance for items to drop as blueprints
  • Pattern Matching - Use partial dino names to match multiple species
  • Player Inventory - Loot goes directly to killer's inventory

docs.title: Commands
docs.text: Command Description DinoLoot.Reload Reload configuration DinoLoot.List List configured dino loot definitions

docs.title: Configuration

  "Settings": {
    "ChatPrefix": "DinoLoot",
    "MessageOnDrop": true
  },
  "DinoLoot": {

        {

              "Quantity": 1,

              "MaxQuality": 3.0,
              "BlueprintChance": 0.1
            },
            {
              "Blueprint": "Blueprint'/Game/PrimalEarth/CoreBlueprints/Resources/PrimalItemResource_Hide'",
              "Quantity": 50,
              "MinQuality": 1.0,
              "MaxQuality": 1.0,
              "BlueprintChance": 0.0
            }
          ]
        }
      ]
    },
    "Alpha_": {
      "Pools": [
        {
          "Name": "AlphaLoot",
          "DropCount": 3,
          "Items": [
            {

              "Quantity": 5,
              "MinQuality": 1.0,
              "MaxQuality": 1.0,
              "BlueprintChance": 0.0
            }
          ]
        }
      ]
    }
  }
}

docs.title: Settings
docs.text:
• ChatPrefix - Prefix for chat messages
  • MessageOnDrop - Show message when loot drops

docs.title: DinoLoot
docs.text:
Map of dino patterns to loot pools:

  • DropCount - Number of items to drop from the pool
  • Items - Array of possible items
  • Blueprint - Item blueprint path (auto-fixes missing .AssetName suffix)
  • Quantity - Stack size
  • MinQuality / MaxQuality - Quality range (1.0 = primitive, 6.0 = ascendant)
  • BlueprintChance - Chance to drop as blueprint (0.0 - 1.0)

docs.title: License
docs.text:
Annual license required.

Purchase at ko-fi.com/rokiarknet

docs.title: EngramControl
docs.text: Block engrams from being learned, and unlock them by killing specific creatures.

docs.title: Features
docs.text:
• Block Engrams - Prevent players from learning specified engrams
  • Kill Unlocks - Unlock engrams by killing bosses or creatures
  • Persistent Unlocks - Player unlocks survive mindwipes
  • Pattern Matching - Use partial names to match multiple engrams/creatures
  • License Validation - Freemium model (5 engrams free, unlimited with license)

docs.title: Commands
docs.text: Command Description EngramControl.Reload Reload configuration EngramControl.Find <pattern> Search for engram names

docs.title: Configuration

  "General": {
    "Debug": false,
    "LogBlocks": true,
    "LogUnlocks": true
  },

    "EngramEntry_TekReplicator",
    "EngramEntry_TekGenerator",
    "EngramEntry_C4"

      "EngramEntry_TekReplicator",

docs.title: BlockedEngrams
docs.text:
Array of engram entry names to block. Use EngramControl.Find in-game to discover names.

Pattern matching is supported - Tek matches all engrams containing "Tek".

docs.title: KillUnlocks
docs.text:
Map of creature patterns to engram arrays. When a matching creature dies, nearby players unlock those engrams.

  • BossArena_ - Matches all boss arenas
  • Alpha_ - Matches all alpha creatures
  • Specific names like Rex_Character_BP_C

docs.title: License
docs.text:
Annual license required for full features. Free mode limits:

  • Maximum 5 blocked engrams
  • Kill unlocks disabled
  • No persistent player data

Purchase at ko-fi.com/rokiarknet

docs.title: ItemExchange

docs.title: Features
docs.text:
• Item Trading - Exchange items at configured ratios
  • Currency Support - Use any item as currency
  • Admin Controls - Configure exchange rates

docs.title: RiderProtection
docs.text: Protect mounted players from taking damage.

docs.title: Features
docs.text:
• Damage Reduction - Reduce or eliminate damage to mounted players
  • Configurable Protection - Set protection percentage per dino type
  • PvE/PvP Options - Different settings for different scenarios

docs.title: Commands
docs.text: Command Description RiderProtection.Reload Reload configuration

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text:
{
  "Settings": {
    "Enabled": true,
    "DefaultProtection": 0.5
  },
  "Overrides": {
    "Rex_Character_BP": 1.0,
    "Giga_Character_BP": 0.75
  }
}

docs.title: Settings
docs.text:
• DefaultProtection - Default damage reduction (0.0 = none, 1.0 = full)
  • Overrides - Per-dino protection values

docs.title: TekUnlock
docs.text: Unlock Tek engrams by killing specific creatures.

docs.title: Features
docs.text:
• Kill-Based Unlocks - Kill bosses/creatures to unlock Tek engrams
  • Radius Detection - All players within range get the unlock
  • Chat Notifications - Notify players when they unlock new engrams

docs.title: Commands
docs.text: Command Description TekUnlock.Reload Reload configuration

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text:
{
  "Settings": {
    "ChatPrefix": "TekUnlock",
    "UnlockRadius": 50000.0,
    "MessageOnUnlock": true
  },
  "KillUnlocks": {
    "BossArena_Broodmother": [
      "Blueprint'/Game/PrimalEarth/CoreBlueprints/Items/Structures/Misc/PrimalItemStructure_TekReplicator.PrimalItemStructure_TekReplicator'"
    ],
    "Bone_Jerboa_Character_BP_AlphaBoss": [
      "Blueprint'/Game/PrimalEarth/CoreBlueprints/Items/Structures/Misc/PrimalItemStructure_TekGenerator.PrimalItemStructure_TekGenerator'"
    ]
  }
}

docs.title: Settings
docs.text:
• ChatPrefix - Prefix for chat messages
  • UnlockRadius - Distance from kill to receive unlock (UE units)
  • MessageOnUnlock - Show message when engrams unlock
